title: dendrocnide diffusa
intro: Dendrocnide diffusa, also known as the giant stinging tree, is a species of plant native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is known for its potent neurotoxin, which causes severe pain and other systemic effects when its leaves or stems come into contact with the skin. While it has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, its potential health benefits are still being researched and its use is generally not recommended due to its toxicity. However, in some traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves have been used to make teas and infusions, often in small quantities and under the guidance of an experienced practitioner.
